title: Standardize AWS Lambda definitions with AWS CDK Construct
description: Use a CDK Construct to standardize and simplify Lambda function definition

introBox.headline: How it works
text: The CDK Construct provides a standardized blueprint for creating Lambda functions (similar to the Global section of an AWS SAM template). This enables standardization of function definitions and centralized updates, e.g., runtime updates. All parameters can be overridden as necessary for individual functions.

snippets.title: Python Lambda Construct Example
snippets.description: A reusable CDK Construct that standardizes Lambda function creation with built-in CloudWatch Log Groups, Powertools for AWS Lambda, X-Ray tracing, and automatic dependency bundling. Supports per-function overrides for runtime, architecture, memory, timeout, etc. The construct can be easily tailored for your own needs. See the repo for a deployable example.

snippets.title: Usage Example
snippets.description: Demonstrates three patterns for consuming the StandardLambda construct: a minimal function with all defaults, a function with overridden runtime and environment variables, and a function with external dependencies, additional layers, and custom IAM permissions.

bullets.text: Working with the AWS CDK in Python
